chunk_summary: This section provides an age-staged feeding prescription template for routine clinic visits, focusing on counseling frameworks for infants and young children aged 0–24 months, with guidelines for complementary feeding and non-breastfed children.
learning_objectives: Understand age-specific feeding recommendations for infants and young children under 2 years.
Learn foundational counseling strategies for routine clinic visits targeting 0–24 months.
Identify key principles for complementary feeding and non-breastfed children aged 6–24 months.
Explore operational guidelines related to infant nutrition and deworming programs.
key_facts: Complementary feeding should begin at 6 months of age, with continued breastfeeding up to 2 years or beyond (WHO).
Non-breastfed children aged 6–24 months require 200–300 mL of animal milk daily, along with fortified complementary foods (WHO).
Routine clinic visits should include counseling on age-appropriate feeding practices and monitoring growth and development.
National Deworming Day guidelines emphasize deworming interventions for children to improve nutritional outcomes (India MoHFW).
Infants aged 0–6 months should be exclusively breastfed, with no other food or liquids introduced (WHO).
reference_quote: Complementary feeding of infants and young children aged 6–23 months is critical for growth and development, with specific guidelines for non-breastfed children (WHO, NBK143674).
document_key_topics: Counseling frameworks for routine clinic visits
Operational guidelines for deworming and nutrition programs
